_comment: PRE-REGISTERED gate compute budget. The critic's point: the honesty machinery (gates) must not starve the training loop. Each gate declares its expected wall-clock seconds, whether it needs a GPU, and which CI LANE it belongs to. The 'fast' lane is the per-PR blocking lane and must stay under fastLaneCeilingSeconds; NO GPU gate may run in the fast lane (GPU gates belong to the 'heavy' lane, run on merge / nightly). These declared seconds are conservative placeholders, NOT measured timings — recalibrate from real CI logs before treating the ceiling as tight.

fast.description: Per-PR blocking lane. Must be quick so it does not starve iteration.

heavy.description: On-merge / nightly lane. GPU gates and long audits live here.
